You need to answer this question honestly, or you will never find any peace.|Dinah Madani and [[Krista Dumont]]|The Dark Hearts of Men}} Madani then got a call from [[Krista Dumont]] who wanted to meet. Madani arrived at Dumont's house. They then talked about [[Billy Russo]] with Dumont figuring that they had a relationship. Dumont was afraid that Russo was going to go deeper and he could not be stopped. She wanted Madani's help.{{Ref|TP209}} [[File:DMadaniKDumont-TalkAboutLogic.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Madani and [[Krista Dumont]] discuss violence]] The two further talked about Russo, but got onto the conversation of [[Frank Castle]] as well. This put the women at odds as they drank. Madani argued that Castle was nothing like Russo, but Madani said that he was still a bad man; the reason was because they both killed people. Dumont argued that Castle should not choose who was innocent or not. Madani rebuttaled that Castle was different because he did not kill women or children. Dumont argued that Castle's outdated belief of chivalry was not enough. Madani backed herself up saying that Castle's code defined him as if he ever killed who Russo killed, he would never forgive himself. They finally finished the talk and Madani left, thanking Dumont.{{Ref|TP210}} ====[[Rescue of Frank Castle|Aiding Frank Castle]]==== [[File:BMahoneyDMadani-ValhallaCrimeScene2.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani and [[Brett Mahoney]] at the massacre]] {{Quote|Why did you help [[Punisher|him]]? In the hospital. You had to know it would end your career. I was planning on coming after you hard."
"I know how you feel. You're in the middle of this... insanity, and you're trying to find some sense of it. But here's what I can tell you. Frank is not a criminal. He's not a hero either. He is just... He's Frank.|[[Brett Mahoney]] and Dinah Madani|Collision Course (The Punisher)}} Madani arrived on the site of a massacre. She was approached by [[Brett Mahoney]], who was angry as he revealed that [[Frank Castle]] killed three women. Madani was shocked and found it hard to believe, pointing at the masks that proved [[Billy Russo]] was also there. Mahoney said it must have been planned as there were rubber bullets used on Castle. Mahoney showed her the death scene with the bodies gone. He admitted that Castle might not have meant to do it, but it still happened. Madani felt awful, which nearly humored Mahoney as he was right all along. She asked if he could get her into Castle's hospital room. [[File:Page and Madani.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani and [[Karen Page]] discuss [[Frank Castle|Castle]]]] Madani arrived at the hospital to find both [[Karen Page]] and [[Amy Bendix]] there. Madani was tired of it all, but talked to Castle, who wanted to be put in jail as he would never forgive himself. Suddenly, Madani got a call from [[Billy Russo]], saying that he was watching her. She put the phone on speaker, which Russo knew she would do. He then scolded Castle before hanging up. Madani then talked outside with Page and admitted that she brought Castle back, so this was her fault, too. However, she believed something else might have happened as Russo would not call otherwise. Page then revealed she could get Madani into the morgue to check the bodies. [[File:Madani learns Frank's innocence.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani realizes [[Frank Castle]] is innocent]] The two arrived at the morgue and Page made a deal with [[Ed Zatner]], one of the employees. He showed them the bodies of the women and explained that there was gunpowder around the bullet holes, meaning the shot came from no more than ten feet away. Another detail was that the bullet was parallel to the ground, meaning it must have been planned and not in the crossfire. Madani realized that Castle was not responsible for their deaths, so she informed Page, and they were both relieved that Castle was innocent. Madani then thanked Zatner for his help and left. [[File:Madani and Frank escape.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani and [[Frank Castle|Castle]] are stopped by [[Brett Mahoney|Mahoney]]]] Madani returned to Castle, only to find [[O'Rourke]], a [[Kitchen Irish]] member, attacking Bendix. Madani knocked him out by hitting him on the head with her gun. Madani then explained to Castle how he did not kill the women, but Russo did. Castle told them to uncuff him so he could get Russo, but they needed a plan. Page pulled the fire alarm, allowing Castle and Madani to get down the stairs as Castle wore O'Rourke's uniform. They finally made it outside, only to be caught by Mahoney, who forced Castle into his van before scolding Madani, saying he would have her fired.{{Ref|TP211}} [[File:Madani shoots at Pilgrim.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani furiously shoots at [[John Pilgrim]]]] As Madani followed Mahoney's ambulance, she called him and told him to stop, but he ignored her. When [[John Pilgrim]] attacked the ambulance, Madani drove straight into Pilgrim's car, knocking him over. Madani got out of her car and searched for Pilgrim, but he managed to get away. Madani hunted down Pilgrim through the stopped cars until she found a woman pointing to her right. Madani turned around to the car on the right, but found Pilgrim had escaped. She turned around to see Pilgrim using her car to drive away, so Madani shot at him, but was unable to hit Pilgrim. [[File:Madani accepts Punisher.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani explains why she helped [[Frank Castle|Castle]]]] Madani headed to where the ambulance crashed and found a badly hurt Mahoney, who was grateful that Castle had pulled him out from the burning vehicle. Mahoney commented that Castle could have left him to die, but Madani stated that Castle was not capable of that. Mahoney questioned why Madani helped Castle, knowing it would end her career. Madani, having been in the same position Mahoney was in, explained that she had now accepted that Castle was not a criminal, nor a hero, but just who he was. Madani was then called by [[Krista Dumont]], who asked about Castle and Russo, which made Madani feel something was off.{{Ref|TP212}} ====[[Attack on Dinah Madani|Defeating Jigsaw]]==== [[File:Madani visits Krista.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani visits [[Krista Dumont]] to investigate]] {{Quote|We got here and you were lying on the floor in a pool of blood. None of it your own. There's blood in the hallway, down the stairwell. [[Billy Russo|Russo]] didn't kill you, but I think maybe you killed him and he just doesn't know it yet.|[[Brett Mahoney]] to Dinah Madani|The Whirlwind}} Madani then did her research and found out that when [[Krista Dumont]] was young, her [[KM|father]] attempted a murder suicide with Dumont. Dumont and her father fell out of the window with the father dying on impact, but Dumont surviving. Madani soon remembered what [[Billy Russo]] said to [[Frank Castle]] about Castle being his own devil. Madani realized that it was what Dumont said to her. Madani then visited Dumont, knocking on her door. [[File:Madani figures it out.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani learns about [[Krista Dumont|Dumont]] and [[Billy Russo|Russo]]]] Madani greeted Dumont despite being very suspicious and asked if she was okay as she sounded off. Dumont blamed it on the panic attacks, saying doctors get them too. As Dumont made tea, Madani brought of the quote about the devil and said Dumont said it first. Dumont deflected this, saying she repeated stuff to her clients all the time. Dumont was still shaken up and excused herself. As she was in the bathroom, Madani looked around and found Russo's notebook with Punisher skulls drawn all over it, confirming he lived there with Dumont. [[File:Madani vs Dumont.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Madani is viciously attacked by [[Krista Dumont|Dumont]]]] Madani immediately pulled out her gun and slowly walked down the hallway. As she approached the bathroom, Dumont lunged at her with scissors, stabbing Madani. The two women fought in hand-to-hand combat. Dumont was getting the better of Madani until she grabbed a cutting board and hit Dumont on the head to get away. In response, Dumont grabbed a knife, lunging at Madani once again. Madani caught the knife and tried to push it away, but Dumont made contact with her face, causing Madani to scream as her cheek was cut. Madani finally got Dumont off of her and pushed Dumont out of the window, falling three stories.{{Ref|TP212}} [[File:RussoJigsawDMadani-AlmostKillingEachOther.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Madani is choked out after shooting [[Billy Russo|Russo]]]] Madani then looked out the window to see Russo with blue flowers in his hands, looking at Dumont's body. Madani quickly took cover as Russo charged in, shooting everywhere in a mad rage. The two former lovers shot at each other. Madani held her ground, shooting Russo twice in the abdomen, but Russo's adrenaline helped him not feel it. He grabbed her and tackled Madani onto the ground. Madani shot once more, putting a bullet through Russo's shoulder. However, Russo still did not back off, choking Madani until they were both knocked out. [[File:BMahoneyOfferingMadanisGunAndBadge.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani refuses to accept her badge]] Madani woke up to find a doctor helping her. Madani freaked out until [[Brett Mahoney]] calmed her down. She immediately asked where Russo was, but he had her sit down, saying how much respect she had earned from him recently. She kept pushing about Russo, so Mahoney explained that Russo was gone. However, there was blood on the wall and down the stairwell, so he was most likely bleeding out and would dead soon. A blood splattered Madani calmed down as Mahoney went to hand over her badge. However, Madani did not take it, as she claimed she was done with it.{{Ref|TP213}} ====Visiting Krista Dumont==== [[File:TP213-MadaniInHospital.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani visits [[Krista Dumont]] in the hospital]] {{Quote|I really did get the therapy I needed at your [[Krista Dumont's Apartment|place]]. Not the talking, so much, but when I finally put three bullets in [[Jigsaw|Billy]], that really helped my state of mind.|Dinah Madani to [[Krista Dumont]]|The Whirlwind}} Madani visited a very injured [[Krista Dumont]] in the hospital and apologized for [[Billy Russo]] getting her involved. However, Madani was surprised when Dumont revealed that she loved Russo. Madani informed Dumont that Russo was still alive, and commented on him abandoning Dumont, although she disagreed. Madani then taunted Dumont by saying that she felt better after shooting Russo. Dumont was convinced Russo would come for her and get revenge on Madani for what she did. Madani then left Dumont, telling her she was responsible for what happened to herself.{{Ref|TP213}} ====[[Execution of Billy Russo]]==== [[File:MadaniMahoneyHoyle-FindingRussoDead.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani looking at the corpse of [[Billy Russo]]]] {{Quote|Was [[Execution of Billy Russo|this]] you? Madani, did you finish the job?"
"I'm flattered, really. But you know what? Yeah, maybe... maybe I did. Maybe I put five bullets in [[Billy Russo|him]] instead of three. And then that's the end of the story. Case closed.|[[Brett Mahoney]] and Dinah Madani|The Whirlwind}} Madani was informed by [[Curtis Hoyle]] that the [[Punisher]] finished off [[Billy Russo]], so she arrived at the [[St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church]] with Hoyle and [[Brett Mahoney]] to see his body. Mahoney wanted to preserve the crime scene; however, Hoyle insisted that every detail about Russo's death was already clear, however, Mahoney expressed his doubt on the situation, as Madani shot him three times, but Russo had five bullet holes in his body. [[File:Madani closes the case.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani takes credit for killing [[Billy Russo]]]] Mahoney asked Madani if she tracked down Russo and finished him off, despite her condition. Madani replied that she was flattered, but then decided to blur the lines and claimed that she could have possibly fired five bullets instead of three. Madani stated that without any possible clues or clear statements, Mahoney could accept this version of events and close the case. When Mahoney asked about Castle, both Madani and Hoyle denied knowledge of Castle to maintain his involvement being kept off-record, much to Mahoney's great frustration.{{Ref|TP213}} ===Employed by the CIA=== ====Recruiting Punisher==== [[File:CIA-DinahMadani.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani working for the [[CIA]]]] {{Quote|So what happened? Get sick of reading people their rights?"
"I guess I spent too much time with you, huh? We need good people here to get things done. And let's face it, when it comes to this stuff, you're the best. I'm offering you a job, Castle.|[[Frank Castle]] and Dinah Madani|The Whirlwind}} As she intended, Madani resigned from the [[Homeland Security]] and joined the [[CIA]], accepting [[Marion James]]' offer. Three months later after her recruitment, Madani was stationed at the CIA facility outside of the [[United States of America]]. Intelligence informed the CIA about high-profile target and Madani was tasked to organize the mission to eliminate him. Due to the level of possible danger, Madani decided that the only person who was capable to complete this mission was [[Frank Castle]]. [[File:DMadani-CastleContact.png|{{AltFloat}}|Madani contacts [[Frank Castle]] for a mission]] However, Castle went off the grid, breaking all his contacts and Madani used CIA equipment to track him down and make contact with Castle. Madani informed Castle about the target that needed to be killed, and Castle noted that she had changed since the last time they met. Madani agreed, commenting that she spent too much time with him. She offered Castle to participate in the mission, doing what he did best. However, Castle dismissed Madani's offer, stating that he already had a job, and ended the call.{{Ref|TP213}} ==Abilities== *'''Expert Combatant''': As a [[Homeland Security]] agent, Madani is skilled in hand-to-hand combat. Madani was able to defeat [[Krista Dumont]] in a close-quarters fight, but was then overpowered by [[Billy Russo]]. *'''Expert Marksman''': Madani is extremely skilled in the use of firearms, particularly pistols, shotguns and assault rifles. She is especially accurate with her sidearm, managing to fire a warning shot at [[Punisher]] at the railing of the staircase he was going down at ''[[Royal Hospitality]]'', just barely missing him. She also killed [[Spencer Geiger]] with her service shotgun during an [[Ambush at the Abandoned Warehouse|ambush]], and showcased tight grouping with the three bullets she put in [[Billy Russo]] and the two in [[Brett Mahoney]]'s vest. {{Quote|So, just curious... did I get anything right?"
"Come on, Stein. It was good. I just made it better. As the SAC should.|[[Sam Stein]] and Dinah Madani|Resupply}} *'''Expert Tactician''': As an experienced [[Homeland Security]] agent, Madani is a competent strategist, demonstrated when she improved upon [[Sam Stein]]'s tactical plan to intercept the [[Greek Mob]]'s weapons shipment. *'''Expert Investigator''': Madani is highly skilled at solving the details of mysterious events that would seem unrealistic to others. She investigated in finding the true cause of her former partner, [[Ahmad Zubair]]'s [[Assassination of Ahmad Zubair|death]] and claimed that [[Punisher]] was involved. Dinah figured that asking [[Billy Russo]] would help so she got [[Carson Wolf]] to arrange a training session at [[Anvil]] to schedule a night out with him and find out about his and Castle's business of what they have done with their group in [[Afghanistan]]. Eventually, after recovering from her car crash from the Punisher, she reported to [[United States Department of Homeland Security|Homeland Security]] to head to the woods to find the corpse of [[Gunner Henderson]], after [[Micro]] sent the coordinates to them. Then she found out that Henderson was one of the soldiers in Castle's group and wonders why Anvil soldiers were present in the woods, she goes to confront Russo at the waterfront, and he lies to her about his unit being out there for other reasons. Later after that, she shortly followed a lead about an arms dealer that the Punisher might be in it and realized that it was Russo in disguise with people in his freelance fighting in a shootout then her friend, [[Sam Stein]], was murdered. When her confrontation with Castle was interrupted by Russo, she realized that he was the masked man who started the shootout then found a bug in her office that was implanted by him since they meant as she also came in conclusion that he was using her the whole time. And was getting everything about her case directly from Castle himself over an interview and found out [[Operation Cerberus]] and [[William Rawlins]] running it all. *'''Bilingualism''': Madani is fluent in her native English, as well as Farsi, though primarily speaks the latter when she is with her parents at home. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== [[File:MadaniP228R.jpg|thumb|250px|Madani aiming her pistol at [[Frank Castle]]]] *'''{{WPS|SIG Sauer P226#P226 Rail|SIG-Sauer P226R}}''': Madani carried her service pistol on her, and took to the ''[[Royal Hospitality]]'' while she tried to speak with [[Billy Russo]]. She became involved in [[Attack on Stan Ori|attack]] planned by [[Lewis Wilson]] inside the hotel, and while trying to help out, she managed to sneak up on [[Frank Castle]], who was attempting to chase Wilson. Madani held Castle at gunpoint while still explaining that she was on his side and wanted him to expose [[Operation Cerberus]], even firing a warning shot while Castle tried to leave. However, Russo shot at Castle, so Madani and Russo held each other at gunpoint until the [[New York City Police Department]] officers then appeared and subdued them. Madani had also led an ambush against [[Billy Russo]] during their hostage exchange where [[Frank Castle]] and [[David Lieberman]] were going to surrender in exchange for the liberation of Lieberman's [[Sarah Lieberman|wife]] and [[Zach Lieberman|son]]. Madani and the rest of [[DHS]] engaged in a shootout against Russo's men, opening fire herself while protecting the two hostages. She took the gun to [[Central Park]] when she tracked [[Frank Castle]] hoping he would lead him to Russo. Upon arriving, she tried to sneak up on Russo, but one of his hostages asked for her help, so Russo quickly shot at her. [[File:The Punisher S2 Trailer 29.png|thumb|250px|Madani aiming her new sidearm]] *'''{{WPS|SIG Sauer P226}}''': Madani started using this handgun as her service weapon once she was promoted to Special Agent in Charge for [[United States Department of Homeland Security|Homeland Security]], carrying it when she inspected [[Arthur Walsh]]'s house in an attempt to locate [[Billy Russo]]. She then found Walsh's corpse, and as she heard footsteps behind her, she turned around and instinctively fired twice against the person behind, who turned to be [[NYPD]] Detective [[Brett Mahoney]]. Fortunately for him, the two bullets hit his Bulletproof Vest. She later took the gun when someone unexpectedly knocked at her door, though it turned out to be Mahoney, and to threaten [[John Pilgrim]] when he started following her to request that she told him the whereabouts of [[Frank Castle]]. Madani drew her handgun when she was chasing [[John Pilgrim]] in a highway to prevent him from killing [[Frank Castle]]. She tried to locate him when he was hiding behind the cars, and shot at him as he stole Madani's car. Madani carried the handgun with her when she went to visit [[Krista Dumont]] when she realized that Dumont had been involved with [[Billy Russo]], and once left alone, began searching for proof. When she found Russo's journal, Madani pulled the handgun, but lost it in the ensuing fight against Dumont. Once she pulled Dumont out of the window, she retrieved the gun as she saw Russo on the streets, and the two of them engaged in a brief shootout. Madani managed to shoot Russo three times, but she lost the gun again as Russo choked her until he too lost consciousness. [[Brett Mahoney]] later returned both the gun and her badge to Madani when she recovered. [[File:DinahMadani-ShotgunBattle.jpg|thumb|250px|Madani shooting at [[Billy Russo]] and his men]] *'''{{WPS|Mossberg 500|Mossberg 500 Persuader}}''': Madani carried this shotgun during an [[Ambush at the Abandoned Warehouse|ambush]] that they had prepared in an attempt to capture the people they were investigating, using the bug in Madani's office to feed false information. As the five men that appeared did not surrender, Madani and the rest of the DHS agents engaged in a shootout, with Madani managing to kill [[Spencer Geiger]]. She went to chase [[Billy Russo]], who unbeknownst to her was the field leader of the mercenaries, but instead, she came across the fatally wounded [[Sam Stein]], discarding her weapon to try to assist him. *'''{{WPS|SIG Sauer SIG516}}''': This assault rifle was the standard weapon carried by Madani, [[Sam Stein]] and other agents of the [[United States Department of Homeland Security]] during a training exercise organized by [[Anvil]]. They successfully shot at the men acting as terrorists, though Stein shot at one of the hostages. ===Other Equipment=== [[File:DMadaniInMicrosHideout.png|thumb|250px|Madani wearing her bulletproof vest]] *'''Bulletproof Vest''': Dinah wore this vest during her missions in homeland security. She first wears it in an ambush against [[Billy Russo]] in disguise with freelance Anvil soldiers, then was seen using one when investigating Russo's former foster caretaker's home. *'''[[Billy Russo's Journal]]''': After Billy Russo's [[Escape from Sacred Saints Hospital|escape]] from [[Sacred Saints Hospital]], Madani stole Russo's journal from his room. Russo took the journal back after confronting Madani at her [[Dinah Madani's Apartment|apartment]]. Madani later found it in [[Krista Dumont]]’s [[Krista Dumont's Apartment|house]]. ===Vehicles=== {{Quote|You know, I loved that car. My [[Hamid Madani|dad]] gave me it to me as a 21st birthday present. Someone killed it. I can't let that lie.|Dinah Madani|Gunner (episode)}} *'''{{WPS|Ford Mustang (first generation)|1968 GT Ford Mustang}}''': On her twenty-first birthday, Madani was gifted this vehicle from her father and drove it to or during work out in the field. However, the car became destroyed after chasing [[Punisher]], who led her to be crashed by [[Micro]]. *'''[[Homeland Security Helicopter]]''': Dinah Madani in her days with homeland security fly a helicopter and was shown flying it to pick up [[Punisher]] after a shootout at the [[Larkville County Sheriff Station]] he requested a ride from her from. ==Facilities== *'''[[Fordham University]]''': Madani attended Fordham University before her graduation in [[2005]]. *'''[[Columbia University]]''': Madani attended Columbia University where she earned a master's degree in Islamic Studies. {{Quote|The way you run this place, pretty cool shit.|[[Wendy (DHS)|Wendy]] to Dinah Madani|One Bad Day}} [[File:The Punisher Oct 27 8.jpg|thumb|250px|Madani arrives in the Homeland Headquarters]] *'''[[Homeland Security Headquarters]]''': Upon her eventual return into the [[United States of America]], Madani was stationed in the [[Homeland Security]] office in [[New York City]]. Madani then resumed her investigation of the [[Assassination of Ahmad Zubair|murder]] of [[Ahmad Zubair]] with [[Sam Stein]]'s help, despite [[Carson Wolf]]'s attempts to stop her to pursue it. In the wake of Wolf's death, Madani was promoted to the SAC of the Homeland Security Headquarters. While their investigation became complicated due to [[Frank Castle]]'s return, Madani and Stein had discovered the bug placed in her office. Eventually, Madani learned from Castle and [[Micro]] about [[William Rawlins]] and the details of the [[Operation Cerberus]]. She brought both of them to the Headquarters where they officially testified against the actions of Rawlins and [[Billy Russo]] while they were under oath. {{Quote|I told your [[Farah Madani|mother]] to keep you home."
"She tried if it makes you feel better.|[[Rafael Hernandez]] and Dinah Madani|Gunner (episode)}} [[File:TP101 MadaniResidence.png|thumb|250px|Madani and her [[Farah Madani|mother]] in their home]] *'''[[Madani Residence]]''': While searching for her place to live in [[New York City]], Madani frequently spent some time in her family residence, speaking with [[Farah Madani]], about her own investigation and her job for the Homeland Security. *'''[[Dinah Madani's Apartment]]''': Now having her own apartment, Madani owns a room that she now resigns while working for the [[Homeland Security]]. Soon, Madani was suddenly called up by [[Frank Castle]] and asked for a place to live with [[Amy Bendix]]. After Castle and Bendix left the apartment, Madani had gained access to her room again. *'''[[Campervan Hideout]]''': Tracking down [[Frank Castle]] by using [[Curtis Hoyle]]'s traces of his phone, Madani came inside the Campervan Hideout and waited talk to Castle about the situation with [[John Pilgrim]] and [[Jigsaw]]. ==Relationships== {{col-begin}}{{col-2}} ===Family=== *Grandmother † *[[Hamid Madani]] - Father *[[Farah Madani]] - Mother ===Allies=== *[[United States Department of Homeland Security]] - Former Subordinates **[[Rafael Hernandez]] - Former Superior and Friend **[[Sam Stein]] † - Former Partner and Friend **[[Bowers]] **[[Sinclair]] **[[Sampson]] **[[Wendy (DHS)|Wendy]] *[[Afghan National Police]] **[[Ahmad Zubair]] † - Friend *[[Micro|David Lieberman/Micro]] - Contact and Temporary Enemy *[[Karen Page]] *[[New York City Police Department]] **[[Brett Mahoney]] - Temporary Enemy **[[Smith (NYPD)|Smith]] **[[Lawrence]] *[[CIA]] **[[Marion James]] - Superior *[[Punisher|Frank Castle/Punisher]] - Former Enemy and Savior *[[Leo Lieberman]] *[[Sarah Lieberman]] *[[Zach Lieberman]] *{{WPS|Office of Inspector General (United States)|Office of Inspector General}} **[[Monroe]] *[[Larkville Police Department]] **[[Roy Hardin]] *[[Amy Bendix]] *[[Curtis Hoyle]] *[[Ed Zatner]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Carson Wolf]] † - Former Superior *[[Greek Mob]] *[[Jack DeLeon]] *[[Jim Pruitt]] † *[[Mike Bashille]] † *[[Spencer Geiger]] † - Victim *[[Tom Weems]] † *[[William Rawlins]] † *[[Lewis Wilson]] † *[[Anvil]] - Former Allies *[[Jigsaw's Crew]] **[[Jigsaw|Billy Russo/Jigsaw]] † - Former Lover, Attempted Killer, and Attempted Victim **[[Jake Nelson]] *[[John Pilgrim]] *[[O'Rourke]] *[[Krista Dumont]] - Former Ally, Attempted Killer, and Attempted Victim {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wikitable" width="100%" ! Appearances for Dinah Madani |- | {{Scroll box|content= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|TP103}} (mentioned; flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TP101}} *{{Appearance|TP102}} *{{Appearance|TP103}} *{{Appearance|TP104}} *{{Appearance|TP105}} *{{Appearance|TP106}} *{{Appearance|TP107}} *{{Appearance|TP108}} *{{Appearance|TP109}} *{{Appearance|TP110}} *{{Appearance|TP111}} *{{Appearance|TP112}} *{{Appearance|TP113}} *{{Appearance|TP207}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TP201}} *{{Appearance|TP202}} *{{Appearance|TP203}} *{{Appearance|TP204}} *{{Appearance|TP205}} *{{Appearance|TP206}} *{{Appearance|TP207}} *{{Appearance|TP208}} *{{Appearance|TP209}} *{{Appearance|TP210}} *{{Appearance|TP211}} *{{Appearance|TP212}} *{{Appearance|TP213}} }} |} ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Aja Frary]] and [[Nitasha Bhambree]] were stunt doubles for [[Amber Rose Revah]] in the role of Dinah Madani. ==Trivia== *Dinah Madani is loosely based on [[w:c:Marvel:Kathryn O'Brien (Earth-200111)|Kathryn O'Brien]], a character from the comics who is also a CIA operative. ==Notes== {{Notelist}} ==References== {{Gallery|Dinah Madani/Gallery}} {{Wq}} {{Reflist}} [[pt-br:Dinah Madani]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:The Punisher Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Fordham University Students]] [[Category:Columbia University Students]] [[Category:FBI National Academy Students]] [[Category:United States Department of Homeland Security Agents]] [[Category:CIA Agents]] [[Category:Sacred Saints Hospital Patients]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]] [[Category:United States Government Officials]]
